stories.headline: Russia urges foreign residents and diplomats to leave Kyiv as it threatens more strikes
stories.summary: One day after one of Russia's worst attacks on Kyiv since its 2022 full-scale invasion, Moscow warns foreign residents and diplomats to leave Ukraine's capital, as it says it does not intend on slowing down its attacks.

stories.headline: Trump tells US negotiators 'not to rush' into deal with Iran
stories.summary: The deal under discussion would involve a 60-day ceasefire extension during which the Strait of Hormuz would be reopened, according to US media.

stories.headline: Iran Says US Deal Not Imminent | The Pulse 5/25/2026
stories.summary:
Iran has denied that a deal to end the war with the US is imminent, after US officials said they were closing in on a deal. Secretary of State Marco Rubio said an agreement could come as soon as Monday, but Iran’s Foreign Ministry Spokesman Esmail Baghaei said “It is true that a consensus was reached on many of the topics discussed, but no one can claim that the signing of an agreement is imminent.”

Turkish police entered the headquarters of the

stories.headline: Moment of gas explosion at China coal mine
stories.summary: At least 82 people have been killed and two are missing after a gas explosion at a coal mine in northern China on Sunday, officials say.

stories.headline: Oil prices fall below $100 a barrel on hopes of Iran peace deal
stories.summary: Brent crude futures down 6% to lowest level in two weeks and stock markets riseOil prices fell below $100 a barrel on Monday and stock markets rose on hopes that the US and Iran are inching closer to a peace deal.Brent crude futures, the global oil benchmark, were down 6% to $97.43 a barrel, the lowest level in two weeks, with hopes that an agreement to end the near three-month US-Israeli war on Iran can be struck. Continue reading...

stories.headline: WHO chief warns Ebola outbreak 'outpacing us' as deaths hit 220
stories.summary: The spread of one of the world's most feared viruses has left responders 'playing catch-up' in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda.

stories.headline: Deal with US not imminent, Iran says
stories.summary: The US secretary of state earlier said that an agreement could possibly come on Monday.

stories.headline: Iran Says US Deal Not Imminent | The Opening Trade 5/25/2026
stories.summary:
Iran's Foreign Ministry spokesman says a deal to open the Strait of Hormuz is not imminent, but that consensus has been reached on many issues. Earlier, oil fell after senior US officials said Washington and Tehran were closing in on an agreement to open the vital waterway.

stories.headline: Russia threatens more Kyiv strikes and tells foreign nationals to leave
stories.summary: It comes after the Ukrainian capital suffered one of the biggest aerial assaults of the war overnight on Saturday.

stories.headline: Pope Leo says AI must be 'disarmed' in first major teaching
stories.summary: The pontiff also warned of a "new digital slaveries" in his first encyclical since becoming Pope last year.

stories.headline: Iran is beating Trump at the art of the deal
stories.summary: The war could end with Tehran more confident, more hardline and with new resources to rebuild its nuclear programme

stories.headline: Pope Leo says AI ‘needs to be disarmed’
stories.summary: Pontiff warns of dangers of a technological revolution driven by ‘the idolatry of profit’

stories.headline: Mexico to Host Iran Players for World Cup as War Tensions Simmer
stories.summary: Mexico will serve as the Iranian national team’s World Cup base after US authorities refused to let the players stay overnight on its soil amid ongoing military tensions.

stories.headline: Belarus opposition leader visits Kyiv after Russia's most devastating attack this year
stories.summary: Ukraine and its allies are increasingly concerned about Belarusian-Russian collaboration, while Moscow has warned that further strikes on Kyiv are coming.

stories.headline: Australian Gaza aid flotilla activists allege abuse after Israeli abduction
stories.summary: Activists allege assaults and torture during Israeli detention after flotilla intercepted in international waters.

stories.headline: Iran says deal with US ‘not imminent’ despite progress
stories.summary: Iran’s FM spokesman Baghaei says a ‘large portion’ of issues with the US have been resolved, but a deal is not imminent.

stories.headline: Spread of Ebola in DRC ‘outpacing’ response efforts, warns WHO
stories.summary: Director general of World Health Organization urges neighbouring countries to take immediate actionThe World Health Organization has warned that the Ebola outbreak is outpacing response efforts and countries neighbouring the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) are at high risk from the disease.“We are urgently scaling up operations, but at the moment the epidemic is outpacing us,” said the WHO’s director-general, Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, as

stories.headline: Surveillance video shows moment of China coal mine blast
stories.summary: Surveillance video caught the moment a powerful explosion surged through the tunnels of a coal mine in China.

stories.headline: Toyota to cut more production abroad due to Iran war
More than 1.5m Muslim pilgrims begin hajj as Iran peace deal hangs in the balance
stories.summary: Despite the uncertainty triggered by the Iran war, Saudi officials noted over the weekend that more pilgrims had travelled from abroad to participate in this year’s hajj than in 2025.

stories.headline: Pope says AI must be ‘disarmed’ to prevent domination, exclusion, and death
stories.summary: In his first encyclical, Leo insists ownership of artificial intelligence data must not be left solely in private hands.

stories.headline: 'Blistering heat': Indians warned to stay indoors as temperatures soar
stories.summary: The BBC's Sumedha Pal described how difficult it was to be standing in the streets of Delhi as temperatures rose to 45C .

stories.headline: Senegal's leadership row mounts as parliament speaker resigns
stories.summary: Some speculate that El Malick Ndiaye stepped down so that ousted-PM Ousmane Sonko can take his place.

stories.headline: BlackRock’s Saigal Sees ‘Sufficient Factors’ to Justify Fed Cut
stories.summary: The Federal Reserve may have enough reason to justify an interest rate cut rather than a hike under new chairman Kevin Warsh, according to BlackRock Inc.

stories.headline: Venezuela inmates occupy prison roof and set fire to mattresses to highlight alleged abuse
stories.summary: Inmates at Barinas prison allege they were peacefully protesting when prison staff opened fire, leaving some woundedInmates at Venezuela’s western Barinas prison staged a protest on its roof on Sunday, piling flaming mattresses and calling for the removal of the facility’s director, whom they accused of overseeing guards as they shot unarmed prisoners.“We want justice. They are shooting us, the guards and the wardens,” a prisoner said in a video

stories.headline: At four, her head was shaved and her clothes burned. Aunty Lorraine doesn’t want her trauma to be forgotten
stories.summary: The now 88-year-old is urging Australian governments to throw their support behind a new national plan for Stolen Generations survivors as they enter their final yearsGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastAunty Lorraine Peeters only remembers the metal gates opening as she was driven away from her home, at Brewarrina mission in north-west New South Wales. She was taken, along with her brothers and sisters, at just four years

stories.headline: Could Brazilian oil emerge as one of the big winners of the Iran war?
stories.summary: China and India are among those ramping up imports of Brazilian crude amid Strait of Hormuz disruptions.

stories.headline: The shrinking, monochrome future of Japan's war-ravaged food industry
Global oil inventories to fall below 100 days of demand on Hormuz blockade
Brazil debt crisis swells with over 82 million behind on payments
Iran delegation in Qatar seeks deal on frozen assets and Hormuz blockade
stories.summary: A high-level Iranian delegation led by parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, FM Abbas Araghchi and central bank chief Abdolnaser Hemmati arrived in Doha on Monday to discuss US accords and the unfreezing of blocked assets, according to anonymous sources familiar with the talks.

stories.headline: Cambodia's former opposition leader receives royal pardon for 27-year sentence
stories.summary: Kem Sokha was serving a 27-year sentence on treason charges, which were widely derided as politically motivated.

stories.headline: Clashes as Venezuelan prisoners protest over alleged mistreatment
stories.summary: The prisoners insisted their protest was peaceful but that security forces deployed to quell it opened fire.

stories.headline: Two LNG tankers pass through Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Another vessel carrying crude oil crossed the vital shipping lane on Saturday

stories.headline: Can Exxon build the world’s biggest carbon capture business?
stories.summary: Oil major bets billions on technology but backlash on US Gulf Coast threatens its ambitions

stories.headline: Bolsonaro film threatens to sink his son’s election bid
stories.summary: Flávio Bolsonaro’s presidential run thrown into doubt by revelations he procured film funding from suspected fraudster

stories.headline: Ghana to Start Buying 30% of Large Gold Mines’ Ouput From June
stories.summary: Ghana’s central bank said it plans to increase gold purchases from the West African nation’s large-scale producers to 30% of their output from 20%, starting June 1.

stories.headline: Emerging-Market Assets Rally as Iran Hopes Boost Risk Appetite
stories.summary: Emerging-market stocks and currencies rose Monday as signs that the US and Iran are nearing a deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz pushed oil prices below $100 per barrel and boosted risk appetite.

stories.headline: Cox Group Took 54% Bridge Loan for Deal to Buy Iberdrola Mexico
stories.summary: Spanish utility Cox ABG Group SA took a 54% bridge loan to help fund a $4.2 billion asset acquisition in Mexico and placed shares as collateral.

stories.headline: Top Bauxite Producer Guinea to Unveil Export Controls in June
stories.summary: Guinea, the world’s biggest bauxite producer, plans to announce reforms next month to control exports of the ore used in making aluminum as it bids to bolster prices.

stories.headline: Nigeria’s First Quarter GDP Eases on Slowdown in Oil Sector
stories.summary: Nigeria’s economy grew at a slower-than-expected pace in the first quarter as growth in both its oil and non-oil sectors eased.

stories.headline: Top Africa Bourse to Tighten Algo-Trading, Market-Access Rules
stories.summary: Africa’s biggest stock-exchange operator is working to tighten oversight of how brokers and trading firms access the bourse to lessen the risk of rogue algorithms and trading errors that could disrupt the market.

stories.headline: Italian Stocks Hit First Record in 26 Years Led by Energy, Chips
stories.summary: Italy’s benchmark equity index rose past its all-time closing high set in 2000, with a recent rally in energy and chip stocks supercharging it to record levels.

stories.headline: Zepto Said to Plan June Public Filing for $1 Billion India IPO
stories.summary: Rapid-commerce firm Zepto Ltd. is preparing to publicly file in the first half of June for an initial public offering that may raise up to $1 billion, according to people familiar with the matter.

stories.headline: Italy Tightens Grip on Payments Company Nexi With Stake Increase
stories.summary: Italy’s state lender Cassa Depositi e Prestiti SpA is set to increase its stake in Nexi SpA, tightening its grip on the payment company considered strategic by Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni’s government.

stories.headline: SoftBank Taps Retail Market Again With $1.6 Billion Yen Bond
stories.summary: SoftBank Group Corp. plans to raise ¥260 billion ($1.6 billion) through another sale of subordinated bonds aimed mainly at individual investors, about two months after a similar retail offering.

stories.headline: India Bond Investors Tap Soaring Swap Rates to Juice Returns
stories.summary: Multi-year high swap rates are driving some Indian debt fund managers into trades aimed at boosting fixed-income returns.

stories.headline: Swiss Trader Had Lucrative Role Getting Iraqi Oil Through Hormuz
stories.summary: A little-known Swiss trading company played a key role in the transit through the Strait of Hormuz of an oil supertanker whose stop-start journey captivated the oil market earlier this month, according to people familiar with the matter.

stories.headline: Trump’s Self-Indulgent Streak Deepens G.O.P. Fears He Is Risking Losses in Midterms
stories.summary: “The stupid stuff is killing our chances,” said a retiring Republican senator.

stories.headline: Will Maureen Galindo’s ‘Zionists’ Comments Matter in Texas House Runoff Election?
stories.summary: Democratic leaders accused Maureen Galindo of antisemitism and are trying to stop her from winning the party’s primary in a contested U.S. House district.

stories.headline: BHP defies its own climate strategy to spend hundreds of millions on polluting diesel trucks in Pilbara
stories.summary: Exclusive: Mining giant says technology is not yet advanced enough to run a fully electrified fleet but experts say it is hooked on federal fuel tax creditsRevealed: the internal BHP memo that slammed the brakes on world’s biggest miner’s climate pushRead more from the BHP files investigation hereGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastBHP has continued to spend hundreds of millions of dollars buying diesel trucks in the Pilbar

stories.headline: Nearly 300 tourists rescued after cable cars halt mid-air
stories.summary: Around 300 people were stranded mid-air after the Gondola system suddenly halted in Indian-administered Kashmir.

stories.headline: China’s Xi praises ‘unbreakable’ Pakistan ties as Sharif visits Beijing
stories.summary: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif says China and Pakistan are 'iron brother' countries.

stories.headline: Cattle market empties as fear grips Eid preparations in India’s West Bengal
stories.summary: Tensions rise in key state, won by BJP this month for first time, as political shift disrupts Eid al-Adha traditions.

stories.headline: Singapore Q1 GDP growth tops estimates at 6% on AI boom
How AI is changing stock trades for retail investors
Japan gold exports hit record $25bn, likely include metal once smuggled in
MENA leaders call for housing to go beyond 'bricks and mortar' at Baku forum
stories.summary: Saudi Arabia, Palestine and Morocco took to the stage at WUF13 in Baku to argue that housing policy must go beyond construction — linking shelter to jobs, climate resilience and human dignity.

stories.headline: Ebola outbreak poses massive challenges, warns nurse
stories.summary: Kate White says she is "extremely concerned about the inability to get resources" to the Democratic Republic of Congo.

stories.headline: Oil Slides as US Touts Progress on Deal Toward Reopening Hormuz
stories.summary: Oil fell as senior US officials gave further, positive signals on progress toward a deal with Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: Man killed in shark attack off Australia's north-east coast
stories.summary: The 39-year-old was fatally injured at a shoal off the Cassowary Coast in Queensland, apparently while spearfishing.

stories.headline: BBC at the site of China's worst mining disaster in more than a decade
stories.summary: At least 82 people have been killed after a gas explosion at the Liushenyu mine in China.

stories.headline: Turkish riot police storm opposition offices after leaders ousted
stories.summary: Officers forced their way into the building days after the party vowed to defy a court ruling removing its leaders.

stories.headline: Blast targeting train kills at least 20 in Pakistan
stories.summary: Officials say the train had been transporting military personnel home for Eid when it was attacked by armed separatists.

stories.headline: Bolivian minister's convoy ambushed while overseeing roadblock clearance
stories.summary: Security forces were met by resistance while attempting to clear highway barriers, amid unrest over the government's economic policies.

stories.headline: Indian billionaires buy foreign companies as growth slows at home
stories.summary: India Inc spent $18bn on global buyouts in 2025 and the deal value could cross $15bn in the first half of 2026.

stories.headline: US Stock Futures Gain as Crude Oil Drops on Iran: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: Global stocks rose to record highs as crude oil fell after officials signaled the US was nearing a deal with Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and restore oil flows. The dollar weakened.

stories.headline: China mine death toll at least 82 after gas blast
stories.summary: Xi Jinping urges authorities to ‘spare no effort’ in rescue operation after explosion in northern Shanxi provinceAt least 82 people have been killed in a gas explosion at a coalmine in northern China’s Shanxi province, in the country’s worst mining disaster in 17 years.The explosion happened on Friday at 7.29pm (12.29 BST) while 247 workers were underground at the Liushenyu coalmine in Qinyuan county, the state media agency Xinhua reported. stories.headline: I survived a missile strike in the Strait of Hormuz, but my friend has not been found
stories.summary: Sunil Puniya was on his first sea job when a missile struck the oil tanker Skylight at the start of the Iran war.

stories.headline: UK officials suggested single market for goods with Europe
stories.summary: Industry figures briefed on the move said the idea has not been taken forward at this point, after EU scepticism.

stories.headline: US navy chief says $14bn arms sale to Taiwan paused due to Iran war
stories.summary: Hung Cao told a Senate hearing they were "doing a pause" on the sale to make sure they had munitions for the Iran war.

stories.headline: US arms sales to Taiwan on ‘pause’ due to Iran war, says acting navy chief
stories.summary: Hung Cao’s comments are latest blow to Taipei after Donald Trump recently cast doubt over US’s enduring supportUS arms sales to Taiwan have been “paused” to ensure the US military has enough munitions for its Iran operations, according to Washington’s acting navy secretary, in the latest blow to Taipei after a series of comments by Donald Trump.When asked at a congressional hearing on Thursday about a $14bn (£10.4bn) weapons package awaiting Trum

stories.headline: PM says Alberta ‘essential’ to Canada as separatists push for independence
stories.summary: Alberta premier calls for referendum on secession after judge ruled initiative to force binding vote invalid The Canadian prime minister, Mark Carney, has said that Alberta is “essential” to the country’s future, hours after the province’s leader moved the oil-rich region closer toward a referendum on independence.Separatists in the western province spent months collecting signatures seeking to trigger a binding October vote on seceding from the

stories.headline: UN’s climate crisis vote shows political momentum is growing, say experts
stories.summary: Resolution backed by 141 states hailed as ‘new chapter’ that could improve climate diplomacy and litigation effortsWhen the UN general assembly voted overwhelmingly in favour of a landmark climate crisis ruling on Wednesday, the Pacific island of Vanuatu’s prime minister hailed the result as the start of “a new chapter” in climate action.“The task before all of us now is to translate legal clarity into meaningful action, stronger cooperation, and

stories.headline: Weather tracker: Heavy rain triggers flooding and landslides in parts of China
stories.summary: Twelve dead as southern and central areas also suffer travel disruption, electricity outages and evacuationsHeavy, prolonged rainfall in southern and central China has resulted in weather warnings for flash flooding, landslides and waterlogging. The slow-moving band of rainfall spanned 620 miles (1,000km) and travelled eastwards across the regions on Tuesday. It was formed from the convergence of multiple bands of rain originating from the Bay of

stories.headline: Rubio tries to reassure Nato allies over US troop deployments
stories.summary: President Donald Trump says he wants to send more troops to Poland, a week after his officials cancelled a similar deployment.

stories.headline: Sabalenka cuts media short as French Open players protest
stories.summary: Leading players, including Aryna Sabalenka, Coco Gauff and Jannik Sinner, limit their French Open media duties to 15 minutes in a row over prize money.

stories.headline: 'No means no': Greenlanders protest against Trump outside new US consulate
stories.summary: The inauguration of the new consulate comes amid a push by the US president for greater control over the island.

stories.headline: ‘Long live Raúl!’ - Castro’s supporters in Cuba rally in his defence
stories.summary: BBC's Will Grant reports from a pro-government demonstration held after the US indictment of the former president.

stories.headline: EasyJet boss says summer flights won't be hit by jet fuel shortages
stories.summary: The airline has seen no problems with fuel, but says people are booking later because of uncertainty caused by the Iran war.

stories.headline: The deadly plane attack at the centre of Castro's indictment
stories.summary: Raúl Castro was armed forces minister when Cuban military jets shot down two civilian planes operated by exiles of the communist country.

stories.headline: Watch key moments from BBC's interview with pardoned Honduran ex-president
stories.summary: Juan Orlando Hernández was controversially pardoned by Trump in December while serving a 45-year prison sentence in the US for drug trafficking.
